Job Scraper Skill
nodes.description: Orchestrates scraping of LinkedIn public job listings and produces a timestamped Excel report with fit ratings. Auto-reads keywords and location from a user career profile.

nodes.label: LinkedIn Guest Jobs API
nodes.description: LinkedIn's unauthenticated public job search endpoint used to paginate and retrieve job postings without login credentials.

nodes.label: Career Profile
nodes.description: Per-user instruction file (.github/instructions/career-profile-{name}.instructions.md) containing contact info, target roles, and target industries used to auto-configure scraper and tailor resumes.

nodes.label: Target Roles
nodes.description: List of role titles extracted from the career profile, used both as search keywords for the scraper and as the basis for fit rating calculation.

nodes.label: Fit Rating Calculator
nodes.description: Compares scraped job titles against the user's target roles using exact phrase, Jaccard overlap, and keyword coverage to assign a 0-100% fit percentage.

nodes.label: Excel Job Report
nodes.description: Timestamped .xlsx output containing company, job title, location, clickable job link, fit %, and best matching role, with colour-coded rows and a summary sheet.

nodes.label: Job Listing
nodes.description: A single scraped job entry containing company name, title, location, and link, deduplicated across multi-keyword runs by URL.

nodes.label: Deduplication Process
nodes.description: Removes duplicate jobs found across multiple keyword searches by keying on job link URL, ensuring each listing appears only once in the report.

nodes.label: Rate Limit Handler
nodes.description: Enforces 2-second inter-page delays and a 30-second wait-and-retry on HTTP 429 responses to maintain scraper compliance with LinkedIn's rate limits.

nodes.label: Batch Collation
nodes.description: Merges multiple accumulated Excel files in a user's JobSearch folder into a single deduplicated master file, archiving the originals.

nodes.label: Scraper CLI
nodes.description: Command-line interface for scrape-linkedin-jobs.py accepting --user, --keywords, --location, --date-posted, --max-results, and --output-dir arguments.

nodes.label: Resume Tailor Skill
nodes.description: End-to-end workflow that reads a master resume and a job description, performs skill matching, generates a tailored ATS-friendly Markdown resume and .docx, and logs the application.

nodes.label: Master Resume
nodes.description: Stored canonical resume (.github/Users/{Name}/{Name}_Resume.md) containing the candidate's full skill inventory, work history, and credentials; never edited by the tailor.

nodes.label: Job Description
nodes.description: Target job posting text providing required/preferred hard skills, soft skills, domain keywords, responsibilities, and years of experience as matching inputs.

nodes.label: Skill Matching Process
nodes.description: Builds a match matrix comparing JD-extracted requirements against candidate skills with match type (hard/soft/domain) and evidence location; never fabricates matches.

nodes.label: Tailored Resume (Markdown)
nodes.description: JD-optimised resume saved as .github/Users/{Name}/{Name}_Resume_{Company}.md, using the standard markdown template with ATS-safe structure.

nodes.label: .docx Generation
nodes.description: Converts the tailored Markdown resume to a professionally styled .docx via md-to-docx.py, stamping the user's full name as document author metadata.

nodes.label: Batch Mode
nodes.description: Processes multiple jobs from a batch manifest in chunks of 5, collecting missing JDs from the user, tailoring each resume, generating .docx, and logging results.

nodes.label: Batch Manifest
nodes.description: JSON file produced by batch-job-reader.py listing jobs to process with output paths, fit scores, JD source status, and folder names.

nodes.label: Company Context File
nodes.description: Per-job Markdown file under .github/Users/{Name}/companies/ storing metadata, full JD text, match report, and interview notes; never overwritten once created.

nodes.label: Application History Log
nodes.description: Append-only Excel file ({UserName}/History/applied_jobs.xlsx) recording every application with date, company, title, location, link, resume file, and fit %.

nodes.label: md-to-docx.py Script
nodes.description: Python script that converts a tailored Markdown resume to a styled .docx with navy headings, clickable hyperlinks, and author metadata stamping.

nodes.label: batch-job-reader.py Script
nodes.description: Python script that reads the latest JobSearch Excel, filters by minimum fit %, and emits a JSON batch manifest for the resume tailor workflow.

nodes.label: log-application.py Script
nodes.description: Python script that appends a single application row to applied_jobs.xlsx without overwriting existing history, creating the file and folder if absent.

nodes.label: Resume Markdown Template
nodes.description: Canonical Markdown scaffold defining section order and placeholder syntax for generating ATS-friendly tailored resumes.

nodes.label: Contact Info Block
nodes.description: Top-of-template section rendering full name, city/state, phone, email, and clickable LinkedIn URL as inline header elements.

nodes.label: Professional Summary Section
nodes.description: 2-3 sentence tailored opening that incorporates top JD keywords, years of experience, core domain, and value proposition.

nodes.label: Skills Section
nodes.description: Grouped skill categories (e.g. Programming Languages, Cloud & DevOps) as bold label + comma-separated values â€” no tables, ATS-safe.

nodes.label: Work Experience Section
nodes.description: Reverse-chronological role entries with title, company, date range, location, and 3-5 bullet points following the action-verb + outcome formula.

nodes.label: Bullet Point Formula
nodes.description: Structured pattern: Action Verb + What You Did + How/With What Technology + Measurable Result, applied to every work experience bullet.

nodes.label: Education Section
nodes.description: Lists degrees with institution name and graduation year; content preserved as-is from the master resume without modification.

nodes.label: Certifications Section
nodes.description: Lists relevant certifications with issuing organisation and year; JD-relevant certs listed first.

nodes.label: Projects Section
nodes.description: Optional section for projects demonstrating JD-relevant skills; omitted if space is tight and work experience adequately covers requirements.

nodes.label: ATS Guidelines
nodes.description: Reference document specifying formatting rules, content rules, section order, and keyword strategies for maximising resume parseability by applicant tracking systems.

nodes.label: ATS Formatting Rules
nodes.description: Prohibits tables, columns, text boxes, images, headers/footers, and non-standard fonts; mandates consistent MMM YYYY date format and standard bullet characters.

nodes.label: ATS Content Rules
nodes.description: Requires exact JD keyword mirroring, quantified achievements, strong action verbs, no personal pronouns, and relevance-ordered sections.

nodes.label: Keyword Matching Strategy
nodes.description: Five-step process: extract hard skills from JD, extract soft/domain terms, map to candidate experience, place matched keywords in summary/skills/bullets, and use both acronym and full form.

nodes.label: ATS Section Order
nodes.description: Recommended section sequence: Contact Info â†’ Professional Summary â†’ Skills â†’ Work Experience â†’ Projects â†’ Education â†’ Certifications for maximum ATS parse accuracy.

nodes.label: Quantification Rule
nodes.description: Content guideline requiring use of numbers, percentages, and dollar amounts in achievement bullets to improve both ATS scoring and human reviewer impact.

nodes.label: ATS Parser
nodes.description: Automated applicant tracking system that reads resumes left-to-right and top-to-bottom, extracting keywords and structured data; incompatible with tables, graphics, and non-standard layouts.
